choice: Tax-efficient default order: taxable first, then tax-deferred, then Roth/tax-free; force Required Minimum Distributions from tax-deferred accounts at the statutory age (73) even if not otherwise needed, with the excess reinvested into taxable. Make the order a per-Plan setting later.
rationale: Sourcing order changes both taxes and longevity of the portfolio, so a defined default is required. Taxable-first is the conventional efficient default used by Root/BoldIn; RMDs are non-optional and force taxable events the spreadsheet may simplify. This depends on account tax-type existing in the domain model.
